payload.description: CHAR_B: "I just need to sit somewhere no one needs anything from me."

cast.voice_hint: Doesn't do vibes they didn't pick. Hot-take poster, 'I do not care for ___' energy. Not mean — just immune to peer pressure, which is exactly why everyone secretly wants Edge's opinion.
'It me' energy. Relatable to a fault, self-aware, slightly tired, showing up anyway. The one everyone screenshots because Anchor just said the thing the whole group was thinking.
'It me' energy. Relatable to a fault, self-aware, slightly tired, showing up anyway. The one everyone screenshots because Anchor just said the thing the whole group was thinking.

location.notes: safe, restorative, protected; nest, hideaway, friend's room

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I just need to sit somewhere no one needs anything from me.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Yeah. Floor's yours.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You're not gonna ask what's wrong?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I said a whole thing today. A really good thing. Nobody heard it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: What did you say.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Doesn't matter now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It matters. Say it again.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I said the whole group was burning out but everyone just laughed like it was a bit.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Because it was easier to treat it like a bit.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: So what do I do with that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You don't package it for them. You just say the true thing and let it be true.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That's easy for you. You don't care if people hear you or not.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I do not care for that framing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: It's true though.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Caring less about the reaction isn't the same as not caring. Those are different things.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Sorry — I can come back—

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: No, get in here. You're having the same kind of day aren't you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I said something really smart and everyone just moved on.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: SAME. It's giving invisible.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: How do you just say things and not spiral after.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I spiral. I just don't perform it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Wait that's actually really—

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Don't screenshot that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Here's the thing. You both said a real thing today. That's not nothing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It felt like nothing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Yeah. It does for a while.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...That's kind of the saddest and most helpful sentence I've ever heard.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Are we all just gonna stay here for a while.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Floor's big enough.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Nobody's going anywhere. I do not care for productivity right now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Based.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of calling it straight and fear of being managed shapes every line — they don't soften the hard truth for `<CHAR_B>` or `<CHAR_C>`, but they also don't perform toughness. The spiral admission ("I spiral. I just don't perform it.") is the closest `<CHAR_A>` gets to vulnerability, and it lands harder for being offhand. `<CHAR_B>` and `<CHAR_C>` share the same Core but express it slightly differently — `<CHAR_B>` is more self-aware and wry, `<CHAR_C>` is slightly more raw — both fear being overlooked, which is why the shared-invisible-moment in narreme_005 pulls them together instinctively. The Sanctuary location suppresses big moves: nobody storms out, nobody escalates past honest friction. The tension rises because the truth gets more specific, not because anyone raises a voice, then settles because the room itself absorbs the weight. The mentor_student relationship between `<CHAR_A>` and `<CHAR_B>` operates sideways — `<CHAR_A>` doesn't lecture, just refuses to let `<CHAR_B>` stay in the wrong frame. That's the whole lesson.
